input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete setup plan for .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## Step 1 — Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Install the latest .NET SDK via winget
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9

# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## Step 2 — Install the MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to confirm the name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install MAUI (includes Android, Windows sub-workloads)
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installed workloads
dotnet workload list
```

## Step 3 — Install Visual Studio Build Tools (Windows target)

Windows/WinUI3 requires the Windows App SDK, which comes with the VS workload:

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or, if you only want the CLI build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required VS workload components:

```powershell
# Discover available component IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" `
  export --channelId VisualStudio.17.Release --productId * --path install

# Install the MAUI + UWP/WinUI components
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installershell.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.ManagedDesktop `
  --passive --norestart
```

> **Simpler alternative:** Open Visual Studio Installer → Modify → check **.NET Multi-platform App UI development** and **.NET desktop development**.

## Step 4 — Android SDK Setup

The MAUI workload bundles an Android SDK, but you may need to accept licenses and verify paths:

```powershell
# Find where the MAUI workload installed the Android SDK
$androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:LOCALAPPDATA "Microsoft\Android\sdk"
if (!(Test-Path $androidSdkPath)) {
    # Fallback: check the dotnet Android manifest
    $androidSdkPath = Join-Path $env:USERPROFILE ".android\sdk"
}
Write-Host "Android SDK path: $androidSdkPath"

# List installed Android platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses

# Install a specific API level (discover latest dynamically)
$available = & "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list 2>&1
$available | Select-String "platforms;android-"  # Shows available API levels

# Example: install API 35 + build tools
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" "build-tools;35.0.0" "platform-tools"
```

## Step 5 — Set Environment Variables

```powershell
# Persist the Android SDK path
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", $androidSdkPath, "User")

# Add platform-tools to PATH (for adb)
$currentPath = [Environment]::GetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "User")
$ptPath = Join-Path $androidSdkPath "platform-tools"
if ($currentPath -notlike "*$ptPath*") {
    [Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("Path", "$currentPath;$ptPath", "User")
}
```

## Step 6 — Android Emulator (Optional)

```powershell
# Install emulator + a system image
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "emulator" "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Create an AVD
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_MAUI" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"

# Verify Hyper-V / WHPX is enabled (required for emulator on Win11)
Get-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName *Hyper* | Format-Table FeatureName, State
# Enable if needed (requires admin + reboot):
Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature -Online -FeatureName Microsoft-Hyper-V-All -All -NoRestart
```

## Step 7 — Enable Developer Mode (Windows target)

```powershell
# Required for sideloading WinUI3/MAUI apps
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /v AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense /t REG_DWORD /d 1 /f
```

## Step 8 — Create & Run a Test Project

```powershell
# Scaffold
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# Build for Windows (WinUI3)
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run on Windows
dotnet run -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Run on Android emulator (start emulator first)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android -t:Run
```

## Step 9 — Discover Correct TFMs Dynamically

If you're unsure which target framework to use:

```powershell
# See what the template generated
Select-String -Path *.csproj -Pattern "TargetFrameworks"

# List installed Windows SDK versions
Get-ChildItem "C:\Program Files (x86)\Windows Kits\10\Include" | Select Name

# List installed Android API levels
& "$androidSdkPath\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list_installed `
  | Select-String "platforms;android-"
```

---

**Summary of prerequisites:**
| Component | Install method |
|---|---|
| .NET 9 SDK | `winget` |
| MAUI workload | `dotnet workload install` |
| VS 2022 / Build Tools | `winget` + VS Installer |
| Android SDK + licenses | `sdkmanager` (bundled) |
| Hyper-V | `Enable-WindowsOptionalFeature` |
| Developer Mode | Registry key |

Adjust version numbers (`net9.0`, `android-35`, etc.) based on what the dynamic discovery commands return on your machine.
data.reasoningText: The user wants a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11, targeting Android and Windows. They can't run commands right now, so they want all the PowerShell commands laid out. Let me provide a comprehensive guide.
